ticket.subject: GLS Integration Roadmap Discussion and API Evaluation
ticket.raw_subject: GLS Integration Roadmap Discussion and API Evaluation
ticket.description:
Hi Team,

We had a discussion with the GLS team today regarding their available APIs, integration roadmap, and market opportunities.

**Key highlights from the discussion:**

• GLS suggested that we initially start with the **ShipIT API integration** and gradually move towards **MyGLS API integration** once the first phase is live and stable.

• Unlike some carriers, GLS does not provide a **single unified API for all countries**. Their integrations are structured based on **country clusters and regional APIs**.

• For certain countries such as **Spain** and other regional markets, GLS also provides dedicated **National APIs**. These National APIs support **additional services and capabilities** that may not be fully available through the Global APIs.

• Because of this, GLS recommended that we evaluate:
- **Global GLS API integrations**
- **Country-specific/National API integrations** where additional services are required


• We had already initiated discussions with GLS around two months ago, and **Manu has completed the Track & Trace API integration**.

• **Shipping API credentials and API documentation** have also been added for technical evaluation.

• GLS specifically highlighted strong market demand for **Czech Republic and Ireland integrations**. According to them, there are currently **very limited or no existing providers adequately supporting these markets**.

• Their recommendation is to progressively support integrations across **all GLS-supported countries**. Once shipment volumes increase, they are open to exploring **deeper partnership opportunities and engagement** from their side as well.

Requesting the team to:
• Review the available GLS APIs and documentation
• Evaluate implementation feasibility and technical dependencies
• Assess support strategy for:

- ShipIT API
- MyGLS API
- Country-specific/National APIs
• Evaluate **high-demand markets such as Czech Republic and Ireland** as potential priority integrations
• Share proposed next steps, implementation effort estimation, and tentative ETA accordingly


GLS is also open to arranging additional technical discussions or walkthrough sessions if required.

Thanks,
Abhilash

comments.html_body:
Hi Team,
 
We had a discussion with the GLS team today regarding their available APIs, integration roadmap, and market opportunities.
 
Key highlights from the discussion:
 
• GLS suggested that we initially start with the ShipIT API integration and gradually move towards MyGLS API integration once the first phase is live and stable.
 
• Unlike some carriers, GLS does not provide a single unified API for all countries. Their integrations are structured based on country clusters and regional APIs.
 
• For certain countries such as Spain and other regional markets, GLS also provides dedicated National APIs. These National APIs support additional services and capabilities that may not be fully available through the Global APIs.
 
• Because of this, GLS recommended that we evaluate:
  • Global GLS API integrations
  • Country-specific/National API integrations where additional services are required
 
• We had already initiated discussions with GLS around two months ago, and Manu has completed the Track & Trace API integration.
 
• Shipping API credentials and API documentation have also been added for technical evaluation.
 
• GLS specifically highlighted strong market demand for Czech Republic and Ireland integrations. According to them, there are currently very limited or no existing providers adequately supporting these markets.
 
• Their recommendation is to progressively support integrations across all GLS-supported countries. Once shipment volumes increase, they are open to exploring deeper partnership opportunities and engagement from their side as well.
 
Requesting the team to:
• Review the available GLS APIs and documentation
• Evaluate implementation feasibility and technical dependencies
• Assess support strategy for:
  • ShipIT API
  • MyGLS API
  • Country-specific/National APIs
    • Evaluate high-demand markets such as Czech Republic and Ireland as potential priority integrations
    • Share proposed next steps, implementation effort estimation, and tentative ETA accordingly
 
GLS is also open to arranging additional technical discussions or walkthrough sessions if required.
 
Thanks, 
Abhilash
